project.name: DummyJSON (MetaCTO Assessment)
project.description: A free, no-auth REST API server that serves placeholder JSON data (products, users, carts, posts, comments, quotes, recipes, todos) plus JWT/2FA auth, an identicon + placeholder-image generator, and custom/mock HTTP responses. Built on Express with EJS-rendered docs, MongoDB/Mongoose for usage stats, and AWS S3 for image storage. Cloned for the MetaCTO assessment.

nodes.name: comment.js
nodes.summary: Comment controller providing CRUD handlers over the in-memory frozen comments dataset, including listing by post, creation with user/post verification, update, and soft-delete.

nodes.name: getAllComments
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated, limited slice of all comments from the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: getAllCommentsByPostId
nodes.summary: Verifies the post exists then returns its comments with pagination applied.

nodes.name: addNewComment
nodes.summary: Creates a new comment after validating body type and verifying the referenced post and user exist.

nodes.name: updateCommentById
nodes.summary: Updates an existing comment by id with validation of body, post, and user references.

nodes.name: deleteCommentById
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a comment by id, returning the record with a deletion timestamp.

nodes.name: http.js
nodes.summary: Controller that returns a mock HTTP status response (or image) for a requested status code, validating against the known HTTP codes dataset.

nodes.name: getHttpStatus
nodes.summary: Validates the requested status code and responds with the corresponding HTTP status payload.

nodes.name: icon.js
nodes.summary: Controller that deterministically generates an identicon image from a hashed seed value with a configurable size.

nodes.name: generateIcon
nodes.summary: Hashes the input seed with SHA-256 and renders a sized identicon via the generator function.

nodes.name: product.js
nodes.summary: Product controller with CRUD plus search, category listing, and category filtering handlers over the frozen products dataset, applying sort, pagination, and field selection.

nodes.name: getAllProducts
nodes.summary: Returns products with sorting, pagination, and selected-field projection applied.

nodes.name: searchProducts
nodes.summary: Filters products by case-insensitive title/description match then applies sort, pagination, and field selection.

nodes.name: getProductById
nodes.summary: Looks up a single product by id and returns a field-selected subset.

nodes.name: getProductsByCategoryName
nodes.summary: Filters products by category name with sort, pagination, and field selection applied.

nodes.name: addNewProduct
nodes.summary: Creates a new product record from the request body, returning the assigned id.

nodes.name: updateProductById
nodes.summary: Updates an existing product by id, merging the request body into the stored record.

nodes.name: deleteProductById
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a product by id, returning the record with a deletion timestamp.

nodes.name: quote.js
nodes.summary: Quote controller exposing list-all, random-quote, and get-by-id handlers over the frozen quotes dataset.

nodes.name: getRandomQuote
nodes.summary: Returns one or several unique random quotes, validating the requested count range.

nodes.name: recipes.js
nodes.summary: Recipe controller with full CRUD plus search, tag listing, tag filtering, and meal-type filtering over the frozen recipes dataset, applying sort, pagination, and field selection.

nodes.name: getAllRecipes
nodes.summary: Returns recipes with sorting, pagination, and field-selection projection applied.

nodes.name: searchRecipes
nodes.summary: Filters recipes by case-insensitive name match then applies sort, pagination, and field selection.

nodes.name: getRecipeById
nodes.summary: Looks up a single recipe by id and returns a field-selected subset.

nodes.name: getRecipesByTag
nodes.summary: Filters recipes whose tags include the requested tag, with sort, pagination, and field selection.

nodes.name: getRecipesByMealType
nodes.summary: Filters recipes by meal type with sort, pagination, and field selection applied.

nodes.name: addNewRecipe
nodes.summary: Creates a new recipe record from the request body, returning the assigned id.

nodes.name: updateRecipeById
nodes.summary: Updates an existing recipe by id, merging the request body into the stored record.

nodes.name: deleteRecipeById
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a recipe by id, returning the record with a deletion timestamp.

nodes.name: totp.js
nodes.summary: TOTP controller that normalizes and validates a base32 secret (or otpauth URL) and generates a time-based one-time password for 2FA.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses otpauth:// URLs and base32 secrets before generating a synchronous TOTP code.

nodes.name: normalizeSecret
nodes.summary: Extracts and normalizes a base32 TOTP secret from a raw key or otpauth URL.

nodes.name: generateTotp
nodes.summary: Validates the normalized secret and returns a generated time-based one-time password.

nodes.name: 2fa.js
nodes.summary: Express router wiring 2FA/TOTP endpoints to the TOTP controller.

nodes.name: cart.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping cart CRUD endpoints to the cart controller handlers.

nodes.name: comment.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping comment CRUD endpoints to the comment controller handlers.

nodes.name: http.js
nodes.summary: Express router for mock HTTP status responses, selecting content type by requested status and delegating to the http controller.

nodes.name: icon.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping identicon image endpoints to the icon controller.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Central router aggregator that mounts all feature routers (auth, cart, comment, products, recipes, etc.) onto the Express app.

nodes.name: post.js
nodes.summary: Express router for post endpoints, mapping list/search/tag/by-user routes plus nested post-comment routes to their controllers.

nodes.name: product.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping product CRUD, search, and category endpoints to the product controller.

nodes.name: quote.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping quote endpoints to the quote controller.

nodes.name: recipe.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping recipe CRUD, search, tag, and meal-type endpoints to the recipes controller.

nodes.name: redirect.js
nodes.summary: Express router defining redirect endpoints for the API.

nodes.name: static.js
nodes.summary: Express router serving static and documentation pages, using shared utilities to load in-memory data.

nodes.name: test.js
nodes.summary: Minimal Express router exposing a test/health endpoint.

nodes.name: todo.js
nodes.summary: Express router mapping todo CRUD endpoints to the todo controller.

nodes.name: error.js
nodes.summary: Defines the APIError class extending Error with an HTTP status code, used across controllers for structured error handling.

nodes.name: APIError
nodes.summary: Custom error subclass carrying an HTTP status code for consistent API error responses.

nodes.name: auth.js
nodes.summary: Express auth middleware that verifies the access token from header or cookie, attaches the resolved user to the request, and clears cookies on failure.

nodes.name: authUser
nodes.summary: Express middleware that extracts and verifies the access token, attaches the resolved user, and clears cookies on auth failure.

nodes.name: clean-request.js
nodes.summary: Express middleware that normalizes and validates query params (search, select, sort, pagination), strips trailing slashes, and processes multipart uploads via Multer.

nodes.name: cleanRequest
nodes.summary: Express middleware normalizing query params (search/select/sort/pagination), redirecting trailing slashes, and parsing multipart uploads via Multer with error handling.

nodes.name: auth.js
nodes.summary: Express router defining authentication endpoints (login, refresh, current user) wired to the auth controller and auth middleware.

nodes.name: user.js
nodes.summary: Express router exposing user endpoints plus the per-user aggregations (carts, posts, todos) by composing multiple controllers and the auth middleware.

nodes.name: jwt.js
nodes.summary: JWT utility wrapping token generation and verification with promisified sign/verify and preset access/refresh token factories.

nodes.name: generateToken
nodes.summary: Promisified JWT signing helper producing a signed token for a payload with a configurable expiry.

nodes.name: verifyToken
nodes.summary: Promisified JWT verification helper resolving the decoded payload or rejecting on an invalid token.

nodes.name: util.js
nodes.summary: Core utility module: loads and deep-freezes all dataset JSON into memory, plus a large set of helpers for subsetting, sorting, pagination, type-checking, slugging, env validation, and push notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses recursive Object.freeze (deepFreeze) to make the in-memory dataset immutable, and promisified fs reads with Promise.all for parallel dataset loading.

nodes.name: deepFreeze
nodes.summary: Recursively freezes an object and all nested object properties to enforce immutability of the in-memory dataset.

nodes.name: loadDataInMemory
nodes.summary: Reads all dataset JSON files in parallel, augments products/posts with slugged data, and deep-freezes the result into the shared in-memory store.

nodes.name: validateEnvVar
nodes.summary: Checks required and optional environment variables, exiting the process on missing required vars and warning on missing optional ones.

nodes.name: isEmpty
nodes.summary: Determines whether a value is empty across strings, arrays, objects, and nullish types using true type detection.

nodes.name: deepCopy
nodes.summary: Recursively clones arrays and objects to produce a fully independent deep copy.

nodes.name: sortArray
nodes.summary: Returns a deep-copied array sorted by a given key and asc/desc order without mutating the source.

nodes.name: getSluggedData
nodes.summary: Maps a resource array to capitalized, slugged display values for a given type.

nodes.name: timeDifference
nodes.summary: Computes a human-readable elapsed-time breakdown between two millisecond timestamps.

nodes.name: sendPushNotification
nodes.summary: Sends a push notification by issuing an HTTPS request with a JSON payload, logging request errors.

nodes.name: sendProcessErrorPushNotification
nodes.summary: Formats process error details with request metadata and dispatches them via the push notification helper.

nodes.name: formatRequestDataForNotification
nodes.summary: Builds a compact, truncated text summary of request method, URL, params, query, and body for error notifications.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Central constants module exporting HTTP status codes, required/optional env-var lists, request whitelist paths, allowed image MIME types, and token/cache expiry settings used project-wide.

nodes.name: auth.js
nodes.summary: Authentication controller handling username/password login and refresh-token rotation, issuing JWT access and refresh tokens from the in-memory user data.

nodes.name: cart.js
nodes.summary: Cart controller providing list/get/add/update/delete handlers, computing product totals and discounts against the in-memory product catalog.

nodes.name: post.js
nodes.summary: Post controller with list, search, tag, by-user, and CRUD handlers backed by the in-memory posts dataset.

nodes.name: todo.js
nodes.summary: Todo controller with list, random, by-id, by-user, and CRUD handlers over the in-memory todos dataset.

nodes.name: user.js
nodes.summary: User controller with list, search, filter, by-id, and CRUD handlers over the in-memory users dataset; the largest controller in the project.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Shared helpers providing user/post existence verification, request-whitelist checking, a configured Multer upload instance, and temporary upload-file cleanup.

nodes.name: loginByUsernamePassword
nodes.summary: Validates credentials against the frozen user dataset, builds the user payload, and returns freshly minted access and refresh tokens with optional expiry override.

nodes.name: getNewRefreshToken
nodes.summary: Verifies a supplied refresh token, re-resolves the user, and issues a new access/refresh token pair to extend the session.

nodes.name: getAllCarts
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated, limited slice of all carts from the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: getCartsByUserId
nodes.summary: Verifies the user exists then returns their carts as a limited, paginated result.

nodes.name: addNewCart
nodes.summary: Validates the user and products payload, resolves products by id, and computes per-product and cart-level totals and discounted prices for a new mock cart.

nodes.name: updateCartById
nodes.summary: Merges existing and incoming cart products into a product map and recomputes quantities, totals, and discounts for an updated mock cart.

nodes.name: deleteCartById
nodes.summary: Returns the targeted cart marked deleted with a deletion timestamp without mutating the underlying frozen data.

nodes.name: getAllPosts
nodes.summary: Returns sorted, paginated, field-selected posts from the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: searchPosts
nodes.summary: Filters posts by a case-insensitive body substring match, then sorts, paginates, and selects fields.

nodes.name: getPostsByTag
nodes.summary: Filters posts whose tags include the requested tag, then sorts, paginates, and selects fields.

nodes.name: getPostsByUserId
nodes.summary: Verifies the user then returns their posts sorted, paginated, and field-selected.

nodes.name: addNewPost
nodes.summary: Validates the owning user and returns a new mock post echoing the supplied body.

nodes.name: updatePost
nodes.summary: Verifies the post exists and returns it merged with the incoming update fields.

nodes.name: getAllTodos
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated, limited slice of all todos.

nodes.name: getRandomTodo
nodes.summary: Returns one or a deduplicated set of random todos, validating the requested count is within range.

nodes.name: getTodosByUserId
nodes.summary: Verifies the user then returns their todos paginated and field-selected.

nodes.name: updateTodoById
nodes.summary: Finds the todo and returns it merged with the incoming update fields.

nodes.name: deleteTodoById
nodes.summary: Returns the targeted todo marked deleted with a deletion timestamp without mutating frozen data.

nodes.name: getAllUsers
nodes.summary: Returns sorted, paginated, field-selected users from the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: searchUsers
nodes.summary: Filters users by case-insensitive match across first name, last name, email, and username, then sorts, paginates, and selects fields.

nodes.name: filterUsers
nodes.summary: Filters users by a nested key/value pair using a dotted-path resolver, then sorts and paginates the result.

nodes.name: addNewUser
nodes.summary: Assembles a full mock user record from a large set of optional body fields, returning it without persisting to the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: updateUserById
nodes.summary: Verifies the user then returns the existing record merged with a wide range of incoming update fields.

nodes.name: verifyUserHandler
nodes.summary: Validates a user id is numeric and resolves the matching user from in-memory data, throwing a typed error when invalid or not found.

nodes.name: verifyPostHandler
nodes.summary: Validates a post id is numeric and resolves the matching post from in-memory data, throwing a typed error when invalid or not found.

nodes.name: deleteMulterTemporaryFiles
nodes.summary: Unlinks each temporary Multer upload file from disk, logging errors without throwing.

nodes.name: findUserWithUsernameAndId
nodes.summary: Resolves an in-memory user matching both username (case-insensitive) and id, used by auth flows.

nodes.name: generateRandomId
nodes.summary: Generates a short random identifier by slicing segments of a UUID v4 into a compact dash-delimited string.

nodes.name: getCartById
nodes.summary: Looks up a single cart by id from the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: getPostById
nodes.summary: Resolves a single post via the verify handler and returns a field-selected subset.

nodes.name: deletePostById
nodes.summary: Returns the targeted post marked deleted with a deletion timestamp without mutating frozen data.

nodes.name: getTodoById
nodes.summary: Looks up a single todo by id from the frozen dataset.

nodes.name: addNewTodo
nodes.summary: Validates the owning user and returns a new mock todo from the supplied fields.

nodes.name: getUserById
nodes.summary: Resolves a single user via the verify handler and returns a field-selected subset.

nodes.name: deleteUserById
nodes.summary: Returns the targeted user marked deleted with a deletion timestamp without mutating frozen data.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Application entry point that wires up middleware, routes, the MongoDB connection, CRON jobs, and fatal/shutdown handlers, then starts the Express HTTP server.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports are aggregated into a single server bootstrap; exports the running server instance for graceful-shutdown handlers.

nodes.name: aws.js
nodes.summary: AWS S3 storage adapter providing upload, existence-check, fetch, and recursive folder-delete operations used as the backing store for the image cache.

nodes.name: image.js
nodes.summary: Express controller for the placeholder-image endpoint that validates size/type, checks the cache, composes the image on miss, stores it, and returns the buffer.

nodes.name: mongoose.js
nodes.summary: MongoDB connection lifecycle module exposing connectDB and disconnectDB helpers built on Mongoose, with error logging.

nodes.name: logger.js
nodes.summary: Centralized logging helper exposing leveled log functions (error/warn/verbose) and a shared logger instance used across the application.

nodes.name: error.js
nodes.summary: Express error-handling middleware that maps known errors to friendly responses, logs structured request metadata, and emits a JSON error envelope.

nodes.name: image.js
nodes.summary: Express router wiring placeholder-image URL patterns to the image controller and supporting utilities.

nodes.name: cron-jobs.js
nodes.summary: Scheduled task setup that registers a CRON job to purge custom responses older than the configured retention window.

nodes.name: fatal-handler.js
nodes.summary: Registers process-level fatal-error handlers (uncaughtException/unhandledRejection) and graceful-shutdown handlers that close the server and DB on termination signals.

nodes.name: image.js
nodes.summary: Image generation and caching utilities: parses size/color inputs, composes images with sharp, derives cache keys, and reads/writes the S3-backed image cache.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sharp for image composition and md5 cache-key hashing; delegates cache persistence to the S3 storage adapter.

nodes.name: request-context.js
nodes.summary: Per-request context store built on AsyncLocalStorage plus middleware to make the current request accessible anywhere in the call chain.

nodes.name: uploadToStorage
nodes.summary: Uploads a buffer to S3 under the given key with the supplied content type.

nodes.name: verifyInStorage
nodes.summary: Checks whether an object exists in S3 storage for a given key.

nodes.name: getFromStorage
nodes.summary: Fetches an object from S3 and returns its contents as a concatenated buffer.

nodes.name: recursiveDelete
nodes.summary: Recursively lists and batch-deletes all objects under an S3 prefix, paging via continuation tokens and logging deletion errors.

nodes.name: imageComposer
nodes.summary: Request handler that validates image parameters, serves from cache when available, otherwise composes, caches, and returns a generated image.

nodes.name: connectDB
nodes.summary: Establishes the Mongoose connection to MongoDB and logs any connection failures.

nodes.name: disconnectDB
nodes.summary: Closes the active Mongoose connection during shutdown, logging errors if any.

nodes.name: basicLog
nodes.summary: Core logging primitive that timestamps and dispatches a message to the underlying logger.

nodes.name: errorMiddleware
nodes.summary: Express error middleware that classifies known errors, logs request metadata, and returns an appropriate status and JSON error response.

nodes.name: buildRequestMetaData
nodes.summary: Assembles a filtered, structured snapshot of request data (method, path, params, body) for logging and diagnostics.

nodes.name: deleteOldCustomResponses
nodes.summary: Deletes custom-response records older than the retention window from MongoDB and logs the outcome.

nodes.name: registerFatalHandlers
nodes.summary: Registers uncaughtException and unhandledRejection handlers that log context, send push notifications, and exit the process safely.

nodes.name: registerShutdownHandlers
nodes.summary: Registers SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that gracefully close the HTTP server and database connection before exiting.

nodes.name: generateProperData
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw image request parameters (size, colors, font) into a validated, well-formed configuration object.

nodes.name: isInvalidSize
nodes.summary: Validates that requested image height and width fall within allowed bounds.

nodes.name: getCustomImageCacheKey
nodes.summary: Derives a deterministic cache key from image parameters using an md5 hash of the rendered text.

nodes.name: composeImage
nodes.summary: Renders a placeholder image with sharp, compositing text overlays and outputting the requested format (png/jpeg/webp).

nodes.name: storeImgInCache
nodes.summary: Persists a generated image buffer to the S3-backed cache under its cache key, logging success or failure.

nodes.name: resolveColor
nodes.summary: Resolves a color input (hex or named) into a normalized hex color value.

nodes.name: getContrastColor
nodes.summary: Computes a readable foreground (contrast) color from a given background color.

nodes.name: requestContextMiddleware
nodes.summary: Express middleware that runs each request inside an AsyncLocalStorage context so the active request is retrievable downstream.

nodes.name: route-stat.js
nodes.summary: Aggregates per-route request counts into a daily snapshot, normalizing/sanitizing paths and persisting the result to MongoDB via the DailyRouteStat model.

nodes.name: normalizePath
nodes.summary: Sanitizes and canonicalizes a request path by collapsing dynamic segments and rejecting suspicious characters so route stats group cleanly.

nodes.name: flushRouteStatsSnapshot
nodes.summary: Folds the in-memory path-count map into normalized buckets plus a weird-paths set and upserts the day's DailyRouteStat document.

nodes.name: user-agent-stat.js
nodes.summary: Aggregates request User-Agent counts into a monthly snapshot, upserting the MonthlyUserAgentStat document and incrementing per-UA counters.

nodes.name: flushUserAgentStatsSnapshot
nodes.summary: Persists the in-memory User-Agent count map into the current month's document, creating it if absent and bumping existing UA counters.

nodes.name: delay-response.js
nodes.summary: Express middleware that optionally delays a response by a requested number of milliseconds before calling next, used to simulate latency.

nodes.name: delayResponse
nodes.summary: Middleware that waits a requested delay via setTimeout before invoking next to advance the request pipeline.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Central middleware wiring that registers security, parsing, request-context, logging and custom middleware on the Express app via injectMiddleWares.

nodes.name: injectMiddleWares
nodes.summary: Registers the full middleware stack (helmet, cors, compression, cookie-parser, request context, custom middleware) onto the Express app instance.

nodes.name: rate-limiter.js
nodes.summary: Express rate-limiting middleware capping each IP to 100 requests per 10-second window, disabled in the development environment.

nodes.name: request-logger.js
nodes.summary: Request logging middleware that times requests, records status/response/total time, and runs a periodic count logger that flushes route and user-agent stat snapshots.

nodes.name: requestLogger
nodes.summary: Per-request middleware that skips whitelisted paths, records start time, and hooks onHeaders/onFinished to log structured timing and status data.

nodes.name: getTotalTime
nodes.summary: Computes total elapsed request time in milliseconds from the recorded high-resolution start time.

nodes.name: startCountLogger
nodes.summary: Starts a recurring interval that flushes accumulated route-stat and user-agent-stat snapshots to MongoDB and logs errors on failure.

nodes.name: set-client-info.js
nodes.summary: Express middleware that extracts client IP/info from the request and attaches it to req before calling next.

nodes.name: setClientInfo
nodes.summary: Middleware that calls extractClientInfo to attach client IP details to the request, then advances to next.

nodes.name: daily-route-stat.js
nodes.summary: Mongoose model for daily route-usage statistics keyed by a day-truncated unique date, storing per-path counts and a weird-paths list.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Schema.Types.Mixed for the dynamic paths map and a unique-indexed date for daily upserts.

nodes.name: monthly-user-agent-stat.js
nodes.summary: Mongoose model for monthly User-Agent statistics keyed by a unique month date, holding an array of {ua, count} subdocuments.

nodes.name: ip.js
nodes.summary: Express router exposing a GET endpoint that returns the caller's extracted client IP/info.

nodes.name: client-info.js
nodes.summary: Utility that extracts the client IP from an Express request, stripping the IPv4-mapped IPv6 prefix.

nodes.name: extractClientInfo
nodes.summary: Returns the normalized client IP from a request, removing the ::ffff: IPv4-in-IPv6 prefix when present.

nodes.name: carts-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: Seed-data factory that reads users and products JSON, then synthesizes randomized cart records (random product selection, quantities, discounts, and totals) and writes carts.json.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Builds one cart per user by picking a random subset of products and computing per-item and cart-level totals, discounted totals, and quantities, then persists the result to carts.json.

nodes.name: getRandomNumberOfProducts
nodes.summary: Returns a list of randomly selected products of the requested length drawn from the source products array.

nodes.name: comments-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: Seed-data factory that maps source comment JSON onto random users and post IDs, assigning randomized like counts, and writes the enriched comments.json.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Loads comments, users, and posts JSON, assigns each comment a random user and post plus a random like count, and serializes the result to comments.json.

nodes.name: posts-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: Seed-data factory that maps source post JSON onto random users and attaches randomized reaction, view, and engagement metrics, then writes posts.json.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Loads posts and users JSON, assigns each post a random author plus randomized likes/dislikes/views, and serializes the enriched posts to posts.json.

nodes.name: constants.js
nodes.summary: Static lookup tables (warranty, shipping, availability, return policies, review comments and reviewer pools) consumed by the products data generator to fill randomized product fields.

nodes.name: products-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: The most complex seed factory: enriches source products with SKUs, slugs, randomized reviews, MOQ, barcodes, and processes/resizes product images and thumbnails via sharp before writing products.json.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the promise-based fs API plus sharp for async image resizing to WebP, orchestrated with Promise.all.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Async pipeline that maps each source product into an enriched record (SKU, slug, reviews, MOQ, barcode, pricing, images, thumbnail) and writes the assembled catalog to products.json.

nodes.name: generateSKU
nodes.summary: Derives a deterministic product SKU from category, brand, and title fragments combined with a zero-padded id.

nodes.name: generateRandomReviews
nodes.summary: Generates a randomized set of product reviews with random ratings, comments, dates, and reviewer identities drawn from the constants pools.

nodes.name: getMOQ
nodes.summary: Returns a minimum order quantity bucketed by the product's price tier.

nodes.name: getProductImages
nodes.summary: Locates a product's source images, resizes them to 1000x1000 WebP via sharp, writes them into the public folder, and returns their URL list.

nodes.name: generateThumbnailImage
nodes.summary: Generates a 300x300 WebP thumbnail from a product's first image via sharp and returns its public URL.

nodes.name: recipes-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: Seed-data factory that maps source recipe JSON onto random users and attaches randomized ratings/review counts, then writes recipes.json.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Loads recipes and users JSON, assigns each recipe a random user plus randomized rating and review counts, and serializes the result to recipes.json.

nodes.name: todos-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: Seed-data factory that maps source todo JSON onto random users with a randomized completion flag, then writes todos.json.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Loads todos and users JSON, assigns each todo a random user and random completed state, and serializes the result to todos.json.

nodes.name: constants.js
nodes.summary: Large static dataset (first/last names, cities, states, companies, departments, banks, crypto, roles, hair/eye attributes, etc.) used by the users data generator to synthesize realistic fake user profiles.
nodes.languageNotes: A 600-line pure data module of exported arrays — no logic, just lookup pools for randomized user generation.

nodes.name: users-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: Seed-data factory that synthesizes complete fake user profiles — usernames, emails, phones, birthdays, addresses, companies, bank/crypto/SSN/EIN details, roles — from source JSON and constant pools, then writes users.json.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Maps each source user into a fully populated fake profile by composing the unique-username, contact, address, financial, and role helpers, then serializes all users to users.json.

nodes.name: generateUsername
nodes.summary: Composes a lowercase username from first name, an initial of maiden/last name, and an optional uniqueness flag.

nodes.name: generateFakePhoneNumber
nodes.summary: Builds a randomized but plausibly formatted fake phone number using a random area code and digit groups.

nodes.name: generateFakeBirthdayFromAge
nodes.summary: Derives a random birth date consistent with the supplied age relative to the current year.

nodes.name: generateRandomAddress
nodes.summary: Assembles a randomized US-style address with street, city, state, postal code, and geo coordinates from the constant pools.

nodes.name: generateRandomCompany
nodes.summary: Builds a randomized company object (name, department, title, address) for a fake user.

nodes.name: generateRandomCardNumber
nodes.summary: Generates a random credit card number string from a digit character pool.

nodes.name: generateRandomIban
nodes.summary: Generates a random IBAN-like string from an alphanumeric character pool.

nodes.name: generateCardExpireDate
nodes.summary: Produces a random MM/YY card expiry date within a future-year range.

nodes.name: generateRandomSSN
nodes.summary: Generates a randomized fake US Social Security Number string.

nodes.name: generateRandomEIN
nodes.summary: Generates a randomized fake US Employer Identification Number string.

nodes.name: getRole
nodes.summary: Selects a user role with weighted probability so most users are regular while a minority are admin/moderator.

nodes.name: utils.js
nodes.summary: Shared randomization helpers (random integers, floats, array picks, barcodes, URL-space encoding) exported via CommonJS and consumed by every data generator in the raw/ folder.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS module.exports object aggregating small pure helpers.

nodes.name: getRandomNumberBetween
nodes.summary: Returns a random integer in the inclusive min/max range.

nodes.name: generateRandomBarcode
nodes.summary: Generates a random numeric barcode string of fixed length from a digit character pool.

nodes.name: cache.js
nodes.summary: Express middleware that caches GET responses by request URL using an in-memory cache, serving cached payloads on hits and wrapping res.send to populate the cache on misses.
nodes.languageNotes: Monkey-patches res.send to intercept and cache the outgoing response body.

nodes.name: cacheMiddleware
nodes.summary: Returns cached response data and headers on a cache hit, otherwise overrides res.send to store the response body in the cache before sending.

nodes.name: custom-response.js
nodes.summary: Mongoose model defining the CustomResponse schema (unique identifier and hash, stored JSON payload, HTTP method enum, access timestamp, creator IP) backing the custom/mock HTTP response feature.
nodes.languageNotes: Mongoose schema with timestamps enabled, versionKey disabled, and a method enum constraint.

nodes.name: custom-response.js
nodes.summary: Express router exposing POST /generate to register a mock JSON response (deduplicated by hash, size-capped at 300kb) and GET /:identifier to serve the stored response with expiry/cache headers via the cache middleware.
nodes.languageNotes: Fire-and-forget updateOne().exec() to bump lastAccessedAt without awaiting.

nodes.name: custom-response.js
nodes.summary: Helper utilities for the custom-response feature: generating a collision-free unique identifier against the CustomResponse model and computing a deterministic SHA hash of a JSON payload plus HTTP method.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node crypto.createHash for deterministic deduplication keys.

nodes.name: generateUniqueIdentifier
nodes.summary: Generates a random identifier and verifies it is unused in the CustomResponse collection, retrying until a collision-free value is found.

nodes.name: generateHash
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic hash from the stringified JSON payload and HTTP method, used to deduplicate stored custom responses.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build on node:24.14.1-alpine3.23 that installs fontconfig and bundled fonts, performs a production-only npm install, and runs the DummyJSON Express app via `node index.js` on port 80.
nodes.languageNotes: Alpine base with explicit fontconfig + COPY ./fonts to support server-side PDF/image rendering; layers package.json + npm install --omit=dev before the full source COPY to maximize build-cache reuse and keep the image lean.

nodes.name: deploy-public-to-s3.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that syncs the public/ directory to an S3 bucket on every push to master that touches public assets, using AWS credentials from repository secrets.
nodes.languageNotes: Path-filtered push trigger (paths: 'public/**') scopes the workflow to asset changes; AWS credentials are injected via GitHub Actions secrets rather than hardcoded.

nodes.name: deploy
nodes.summary: Single ubuntu-latest job that checks out the repo, installs the AWS CLI, and runs an aws s3 sync to publish the public/ folder to the S3 bucket (us-east-2) with --delete.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template documenting the runtime configuration the DummyJSON server expects: NODE_ENV, JWT_SECRET for auth token signing, MONGODB_URI and MONGODB_DB_NAME for the database connection, and LOG_ENABLED for logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Copied to .env at setup time; the dev script loads it via env-cmd. JWT_SECRET and MONGODB_URI are sensitive and must be supplied per-environment, never committed.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and usage documentation for the DummyJSON free no-auth REST API, covering why to use it, how to fetch data, available resources, query features (filtering, pagination, nested resources, delay), the identicon and dummy image generators, supported fonts, contributors, and the MIT license.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js project manifest for the DummyJSON server (ESM, main entry index.js, Node >=24). Declares start/dev/minify scripts and runtime dependencies including Express, Mongoose/MongoDB, AWS S3 client, EJS, helmet, jsonwebtoken, sharp, and winston, plus ESLint/Prettier/nodemon dev tooling.
nodes.languageNotes: "type": "module" marks the project as ESM. Pins several security-relevant deps (helmet, jsonwebtoken, otplib) and a strict engines.node >=24 requirement.

nodes.name: _404.scss
nodes.summary: SCSS partial styling the 404 error page, including the centered layout, gradient background, floating artwork animation, badge, and action buttons. Relies on color variables and the breakpoint mixin defined in _base.scss.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses BEM-style nesting (&__glow, &__inner), the shared breakpoint() mixin, and a @keyframes float animation; references $primary-color/$primary-color-dark without a direct @import.

nodes.name: _base.scss
nodes.summary: Foundational SCSS partial defining the design tokens (breakpoint map, color palette, nav height), the breakpoint() responsive mixin, global resets, and shared element/button styles used across the site.
nodes.languageNotes: Declares the $breakpoints map plus map-has-key/map-get-driven breakpoint() mixin and color/$nav_height variables consumed by the other partials; imports the font-face partial.

nodes.name: _font-dm-sans.scss
nodes.summary: SCSS partial registering the DM Sans typeface via five @font-face declarations (weights 100-900) pointing at local .ttf files under /public/fonts/DM_Sans.

nodes.name: _nav.scss
nodes.summary: SCSS partial styling the fixed glassmorphic navbar, responsive burger menu, nav items, and the announcement banner, with breakpoint-driven behavior. Imports _base.scss for tokens and the breakpoint mixin.
nodes.languageNotes: Glassmorphism via backdrop-filter, nested BEM-ish selectors, and the shared breakpoint() mixin at xs/sm/md; uses $nav_height and $primary-color from _base.scss.

nodes.name: FUNDING.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub funding configuration declaring sponsorship channels (GitHub Sponsors and Buy Me a Coffee) shown on the repository.

nodes.name: fonts.conf
nodes.summary: Fontconfig file declaring custom font directories so the server can locate bundled fonts (used for identicon/placeholder image generation).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal README note for the fonts directory.

nodes.name: docs.scss
nodes.summary: Stylesheet for the documentation pages of the DummyJSON docs site, covering the docs layout, sidebar navigation, code blocks, and responsive behavior.
nodes.languageNotes: SCSS with nested selectors and variables driving the docs page theme.

nodes.name: styles.scss
nodes.summary: Primary global stylesheet for the DummyJSON site, defining the layout, navigation, hero, resource explorer, custom-response builder, and responsive breakpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Large SCSS file (~1000 non-empty lines) with extensive nesting and shared variables/mixins.

nodes.name: 2fa.js
nodes.summary: Browser script powering the 2FA/TOTP demo page: generates TOTP codes via the API, copies them to clipboard, and runs a live countdown timer that auto-refreshes the code.

nodes.name: common.js
nodes.summary: Shared browser utilities used across docs pages: mobile nav menu toggling and a clipboard copy helper.

nodes.name: custom-json-highlight.js
nodes.summary: Client-side JSON pretty-printer and syntax highlighter that recursively formats objects, arrays, and values into highlighted HTML.

nodes.name: custom-response.js
nodes.summary: Browser script for the custom/mock-response builder: handles method selection, editable JSON code input, prettify, URL copy, and posting the custom API definition to generate a mock endpoint.

nodes.name: docs.js
nodes.summary: Documentation page behavior: code block formatting and Prism highlighting, show-output toggles, sidebar nav link routing, and active-section navigation syncing.

nodes.name: script.js
nodes.summary: Home page browser script: highlights the try-it JSON block, drives the resource selector that fetches sample data, and wires arrow-icon section scrolling.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Robots exclusion file directing crawler access and pointing to the sitemap for the DummyJSON site.

nodes.name: sitemap.xml
nodes.summary: XML sitemap enumerating the documentation and resource pages of the DummyJSON site for search engine indexing.

nodes.name: quotes-data-generator.js
nodes.summary: One-off Node script that reads raw quotes JSON and rewrites it into the seed data structure used by the quotes resource.

nodes.name: loadtest.js
nodes.summary: k6 load-testing script defining virtual-user stages and a request helper that hits API endpoints and checks responses.
nodes.languageNotes: k6 script exporting an options object plus a default VU function; runs under the k6 runtime, not Node.

nodes.name: minify-and-watch-public-js-files.js
nodes.summary: Build/dev script that minifies the public browser JS files with UglifyJS and watches them for changes during development.

nodes.name: http-codes.js
nodes.summary: Constants module mapping HTTP status code names to their numeric values for use across the server.

nodes.name: 2fa.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS template for the 2FA/TOTP demo page, rendering the key input, generate button, code display, and countdown timer wired up by 2fa.js.

nodes.name: 404.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS template for the 404 not-found error page of the docs site.

nodes.name: custom-response.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS template for the custom/mock-response builder page, rendering the method selector, editable JSON editor, and generate controls driven by custom-response.js.

nodes.name: docs-auth.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation template describing the authentication (JWT/login) endpoints with request and response examples.

nodes.name: docs-carts.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation template covering the carts resource endpoints with request/response examples and code snippets.

nodes.name: docs-comments.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation template covering the comments resource endpoints with request/response examples and code snippets.

nodes.name: docs-http.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation template for the custom HTTP-response feature, explaining how to return arbitrary status codes and messages.

nodes.name: docs-image.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation template for the image generator endpoints (identicon/placeholder images) with usage examples and parameters.

nodes.name: handleTotpCopyClick
nodes.summary: Wires the copy button on the TOTP page to select the code input and copy it to the clipboard with a transient confirmation state.

nodes.name: generateTotpCode
nodes.summary: Validates the secret key input and POSTs it to the API to generate a TOTP code, then displays the result or an error.

nodes.name: startTotpCountdown
nodes.summary: Runs the live countdown/progress animation for the current TOTP code and auto-regenerates it when the interval expires.

nodes.name: clearTotpCountdown
nodes.summary: Tears down the TOTP countdown timers and intervals and resets the timer UI state.

nodes.name: handleNavMenuClickOnMobile
nodes.summary: Toggles the mobile burger navigation menu and closes it when clicking outside the nav.

nodes.name: highlightJSON
nodes.summary: Recursively formats a JSON value into indented, syntax-highlighted HTML by dispatching to internal array/object/value formatters.

nodes.name: handleHomePageMethodSelector
nodes.summary: Manages the HTTP method dropdown selector on the custom-response builder, toggling the menu and reflecting the chosen method.

nodes.name: handlePreCodeInput
nodes.summary: Makes the JSON code block editable, re-highlighting on input and handling Tab/Enter/paste to keep editing behavior sane.

nodes.name: handlePrettifyJSONCode
nodes.summary: Wires the prettify button to parse and re-highlight the editor's JSON, alerting on invalid input.

nodes.name: handleCopyURLOnInputClick
nodes.summary: Copies the generated custom-response URL to the clipboard with a transient confirmation state when the input is clicked.

nodes.name: generateCustomAPI
nodes.summary: Validates the custom JSON payload and POSTs the method/status/response to the API to create a mock endpoint, then surfaces the resulting URL.

nodes.name: formatCode
nodes.summary: Normalizes indentation of code blocks matching a selector by stripping common leading whitespace for clean display.

nodes.name: highlightJSCodeBlocks
nodes.summary: Polls until code blocks have language classes, then triggers Prism syntax highlighting across the docs page.

nodes.name: addListenerOnShowOutputButton
nodes.summary: Attaches click handlers to show/hide the example output panel for each documented endpoint.

nodes.name: handleDocLinksClick
nodes.summary: Handles sidebar documentation link clicks: expands parent menus, manages active states, and performs in-page navigation between doc sections.

nodes.name: replaceInlineCode
nodes.summary: Replaces backtick-wrapped inline markup in matched elements with styled inline code spans.

nodes.name: selectNavItem
nodes.summary: Resolves the nav item for a URL hash and marks it plus its parent menu active to sync sidebar state with the current section.

nodes.name: handleHomePageResourcesSelector
nodes.summary: Drives the home page resource dropdown, fetching sample JSON for the selected resource and rendering it in the try-it block.

nodes.name: handleSectionScrollViaArrowIcons
nodes.summary: Wires arrow icons to smoothly scroll the page to the next/previous section.

nodes.name: generateData
nodes.summary: Reads raw quotes JSON, maps each entry into the seed data shape, and writes the transformed dataset back to disk.

nodes.name: minifyJS
nodes.summary: Reads a public JS file, minifies it with UglifyJS, and writes the .min.js output, logging any minification errors.

nodes.name: docs-posts.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the Posts resource of the DummyJSON API, rendering endpoint descriptions, request/response examples, and code samples for posts, comments, and reactions.
nodes.languageNotes: Composes shared partials (docs-head, header, docs-side-nav, docs-footer) via EJS include directives.

nodes.name: docs-products.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the Products resource, documenting product listing, search, category, and CRUD endpoints with example payloads.

nodes.name: docs-quotes.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the Quotes resource, describing quote listing, single quote, and random quote endpoints.

nodes.name: docs-recipes.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the Recipes resource, documenting recipe listing, search, tag/meal-type filters, and detail endpoints with example responses.

nodes.name: docs-todos.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the Todos resource, describing todo listing, random, per-user, and CRUD endpoints.

nodes.name: docs-tools.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the API utility tools, covering the identicon/placeholder image generator and custom/mock HTTP response endpoints.

nodes.name: docs-users.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS documentation page for the Users resource, the largest docs view, documenting user listing, search, filter, per-resource lookups, and CRUD endpoints.

nodes.name: docs.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS landing/overview page for the API documentation section, introducing the docs site and linking out to per-resource pages.

nodes.name: index.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS home/landing page for the DummyJSON site, presenting the project overview, feature highlights, and quick-start example outside the docs layout.

nodes.name: docs-footer.ejs
nodes.summary: Shared EJS footer partial included at the bottom of every documentation page, rendering footer links and closing markup.

nodes.name: docs-head.ejs
nodes.summary: Shared EJS head partial included at the top of every documentation page, providing the HTML head, meta tags, and stylesheet/script links.

nodes.name: docs-side-nav.ejs
nodes.summary: Shared EJS sidebar navigation partial included on every documentation page, listing links to all API resource and tool docs sections.

nodes.name: header.ejs
nodes.summary: Shared EJS header/top-bar partial included across docs pages and the home/tools pages, rendering site branding and top-level navigation.

nodes.name: tools.ejs
nodes.summary: EJS page for the API tools showcase, presenting the image generator and custom-response utilities outside the per-resource docs layout.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Express route definitions, controllers, and middleware that handle HTTP requests, validation, and response rendering for the DummyJSON endpoints, plus the index.js app bootstrap.

layers.name: Business Logic
layers.description: Domain helpers implementing core behaviors like JWT/2FA auth, identicon/placeholder image generation, and custom/mock HTTP response logic.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Mongoose models for usage stats, the MongoDB connection module, and the AWS S3 client used for image persistence.

layers.name: Utility Layer
layers.description: Shared cross-cutting helpers and constants (formatting, query parsing, pagination, regex/config constants) consumed across the API.

layers.name: Views (Docs Site)
layers.description: EJS templates and partials that render the documentation website, error pages, and interactive tool pages.

layers.name: Static Assets
layers.description: Browser-facing CSS/SCSS, client-side JS, fonts, robots.txt and sitemap.xml served for the docs site.

layers.name: Seed Data Generators
layers.description: raw/ scripts and datasets that generate the placeholder JSON served by the API (products, users, carts, posts, comments, quotes, recipes, todos).

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Dockerfile, GitHub Actions deploy workflows, and build/loadtest tooling scripts.

layers.name: Configuration & Documentation
layers.description: Project-level configuration and documentation: package.json, .env.example, README, and funding metadata.

tour.title: Project Overview & Setup
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what DummyJSON is: a free, no-auth REST API that serves placeholder JSON for products, users, carts, posts, comments, quotes, recipes, and todos, plus JWT/2FA auth, an image generator, and custom mock responses. The package.json manifest confirms the stack the rest of the tour explores: an ESM Node app on Express with Mongoose/MongoDB, the AWS S3 client, EJS for docs, sharp for images, helmet, jsonwebtoken, and winston. The .env.example documents the four things the server needs to run: NODE_ENV, JWT_SECRET, the MongoDB connection, and LOG_ENABLED.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: index.js is where everything is wired together and the server comes alive. It connects to MongoDB, injects the middleware stack, mounts the route aggregator, schedules CRON jobs, registers fatal/shutdown handlers, and finally starts the Express HTTP server. Read this first to get the bird's-eye view of the runtime before zooming into each subsystem.
tour.languageLesson: This project uses ESM ("type": "module" in package.json), so it imports with `import ... from` rather than CommonJS `require`. Note the raw/ seed generators are the exception — they use CommonJS, which is why they live in their own folder.

tour.title: The Request Pipeline: Middleware
tour.description: Before any request reaches a controller it passes through the middleware stack assembled in middleware/index.js. This is where security, body parsing, the per-request context (an AsyncLocalStorage store that makes the current request reachable anywhere), and logging are registered. Building on the entry point from Step 2, this step shows how index.js delegates cross-cutting concerns to a single injectMiddleWares call.
tour.languageLesson: Express middleware is the chain-of-responsibility pattern: each function receives (req, res, next) and either responds or calls next() to pass control along. Ordering matters — security and parsing must run before the handlers that depend on them.

tour.title: Auth, Cleaning & Rate Limiting Middleware
tour.description: These four middleware enforce the request contract. auth.js verifies the JWT access token (from header or cookie) and attaches the resolved user; clean-request.js normalizes and validates query params (search, select, sort, pagination), strips trailing slashes, and handles multipart uploads via Multer; rate-limiter.js caps each IP at 100 requests per 10-second window; and delay-response.js lets a caller inject artificial latency to simulate slow networks — the 'delay' feature the README advertises.

tour.title: Routing: From URL to Handler
tour.description: routes/index.js is the central aggregator — the highest fan-out file in the codebase — mounting every feature router (products, users, carts, posts, comments, quotes, recipes, todos, auth, image, and more) onto the app. The resource routers map REST URLs to controller handlers; user.js is the richest, composing several controllers to expose per-user aggregations like a user's carts, posts, and todos.

tour.title: Controllers: The Resource Logic
tour.description: Controllers hold the per-resource behavior the routes delegate to. Each one reads from an in-memory, deep-frozen dataset and applies search, sorting, pagination, and field selection. product.js and user.js (the largest controller) are representative CRUD-plus-query handlers, while cart.js is more interesting: it computes product totals and discounts against the in-memory product catalog, showing how resources cross-reference each other.

tour.title: Authentication & 2FA
tour.description: This is the auth path the README highlights. The auth controller handles username/password login and refresh-token rotation, issuing JWT access and refresh tokens via the jwt utility (promisified sign/verify with preset token factories). The TOTP controller validates a base32 secret or otpauth URL and generates a time-based one-time password, powering the 2FA demo. Together with the auth middleware from Step 4, this completes the full login lifecycle.
tour.languageLesson: JWTs are stateless: the server signs claims with JWT_SECRET and trusts any token whose signature verifies, so no session store is needed. Access tokens are short-lived; the refresh token rotates them — which is exactly the flow auth.js implements.

tour.title: Shared Utilities & Constants
tour.description: These are the most-depended-upon files in the project (util.js has the highest fan-in, 21). util.js loads and deep-freezes every dataset JSON into memory and provides the subsetting, sorting, pagination, type-checking, and slugging helpers every controller calls. error.js defines the APIError class used for structured errors, constants/index.js centralizes HTTP codes, env-var lists, request whitelists, and expiry settings, and logger.js is the shared winston logger. Understanding these explains how all the previous steps actually do their work.
tour.languageLesson: Object.freeze (used recursively as a 'deep freeze' here) makes the in-memory datasets immutable, so the no-auth public API can never be corrupted by a request — every write returns a simulated result without mutating the shared data.

tour.title: Image Generation & AWS S3
tour.description: The image features from the README — identicons and placeholder images — live here. The image controller validates size/type, checks the cache, composes the image on a miss, stores it, and returns the buffer; utils/image.js does the sharp-based composition and cache-key derivation; and aws/aws.js is the S3 adapter (upload, existence-check, fetch, recursive delete) that backs the image cache. This is the project's one persistent binary store, distinct from the in-memory JSON datasets.

tour.title: Custom Responses & Usage Stats (MongoDB)
tour.description: Here is the only place the app writes to MongoDB. The custom-response feature lets users POST a mock JSON payload and retrieve it by identifier — its Mongoose model stores the payload, a dedupe hash, the HTTP method, and a CRON job purges old entries. The other two models capture observability: daily-route-stat and monthly-user-agent-stat aggregate per-route and per-user-agent request counts, flushed periodically by the request logger from Step 3.
tour.languageLesson: Mongoose models map a schema to a MongoDB collection. The stat models use a unique day/month-truncated date key with upserts so each period gets exactly one document that counters increment into — a common time-bucketed aggregation pattern.

tour.title: Seed Data Generators
tour.description: The placeholder JSON the API serves isn't hand-written — it's synthesized by the raw/ generators. raw/utils.js provides the shared randomization helpers every generator uses. users-data-generator.js fabricates complete fake profiles (emails, addresses, banks, crypto, roles) from constant pools, and products-data-generator.js is the most complex: it enriches products with SKUs, slugs, reviews, and barcodes and resizes images via sharp. These scripts produce the database/*.json that util.js (Step 8) loads at startup.

tour.title: The Docs Site: EJS Views & Assets
tour.description: DummyJSON ships its own documentation website, server-rendered with EJS. docs.ejs is the docs hub, the shared partials (header, head, side-nav, footer) are included on every page giving them high fan-in, and per-resource pages like docs-products.ejs document each endpoint with live examples. The browser side is driven by the public/ assets: styles.scss for the global look and script.js for the interactive resource explorer on the home page.
tour.languageLesson: EJS partials (<%- include('partials/header') %>) are server-side composition: shared markup is factored into one file and embedded at render time, which is why the partials show such high fan-in across the docs pages.

tour.title: Infrastructure: Docker & CI/CD
tour.description: Finally, how the app ships. The Dockerfile builds on node:24-alpine, installs fontconfig and the bundled fonts (needed for identicon/image text rendering from Step 9), runs a production-only npm install, and starts the app with `node index.js` on port 80. The GitHub Actions workflow handles the static side: on every push to master touching public assets, it syncs the public/ directory to an S3 bucket so the docs site's CSS/JS are served from S3.
tour.languageLesson: This is a single-stage Dockerfile (one FROM). It's acceptable here because the app is plain JS with no compile step — but installing fonts into the image is the load-bearing detail, since the placeholder-image generator shells out to fontconfig at runtime.
